LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANTS EDWIN LOW, CONNIE LOW YAMAMOTO AND JENNIFER HOVLAND'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE NINTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NUISANCE. SUSANNE HAS NOT ADEQUATELY PLED NUISANCE BASED ON HER ALLEGATIONS OF BEING LOCKED OUT. PHILLIPS V. CITY OF PASADENA (1945) 27 CAL.2D 104 IS DISTINGUISHABLE AS IT INVOLVED ACCESS TO A ROAD. NOR IS THERE A REASONABLE POSSIBILITY THAT SUSANNE CAN CURE THIS DEFICIENCY BY AN AMENDMENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED. =302/HK

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. CROSS DEFENDANTS EDWIN LOW, CONNIE LOW YAMAMOTO AND JENNIFER HOVLAND'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH FIFTEEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR WASTE AND OVERRULED AS TO ALL OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION CHALLENGED BY THE DEMURRER. SUSANNE IS GRANTED TO LEAVE TO ALLEGE, IF SHE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, THAT EDWIN SUBSTANTIALLY OR PERMANENTLY DIMINISHED OR DEPRECIATED THE MARKET VALUE OF THE PROPERTY. ALL OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION ARE ADEQUATELY PLED AND ARE NOT UNCERTAIN. THE COURT TAKES JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE EXISTENCE AND FILING OF THE DOCUMENTS SOUGHT TO BE JUDICIALLY NOTICED, BUT NOT THE TRUTH OF THE CONTENTS OF THOSE DOCUMENTS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR #5976

REPLY DECLARATION OF WARREN T WOO, ESQ IN SUPPORT OF PLTF'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT AND TO ENFORCE THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT AGAINST DEFT SUSANNE LOW [CCP 664.6] FILED BY PLAINTIFF LOW, EDWIN YAMAMOTO, CONNIE LOW HOVLAND, JENNIFER

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFFS EDWIN LOW, CONNIE LOW YAMAMOTO AND JENNIFER HOVLAND'S MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AND FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT AGAINST SUSANNE LOW [CCP 664.6] MOTION FOR JUDGMENT AND TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 664.6. THE WRITTEN STIPULATION FOR SETTLEMENT WAS AGREED TO BETWEEN THE PARTIES AND PROVIDED THAT IT WAS "BINDING AND MAY BE ENFORCED BY A MOTION UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 664.6 OR BY ANY OTHER PROCEDURE PERMITTED BY LAW IN THE APPLICABLE STATE OR FEDERAL COURT." DEFENDANT SUZANNE LOW FAILED TO COMPLY WITH THE TERMS OF THE STIPULATION FOR SETTLEMENT WHEN SHE FAILED TO SIGN AND DELIVER A GRANT DEED OF HER 8 AND 1/3% INTEREST IN PROPERTY 809 COLUMBUS TO PLAINTIFF EDWIN LOW. (DECL. OF EDWIN LOW, EXH. A1 - A4.) PLAINTIFFS SATISFIED THEIR OBLIGATIONS UNDER THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT BY MAKING AN INITIAL $60,000 PAYMENT (DECL. OF WOO IN SUPPORT OF MOTION, 12-19), PROVIDING RECORDS RECORDED BY MR. WOO ON THE PROPERTY TO MR. SHEPPARD (DECL. OF WOO IN SUPPORT OF REPLY TO THE OPPOSITION, 28), AND BY BRINGING A CASHIERS' CHECK FOR $100,000 TO THE DECEMBER 10, 2012 AND MARCH 18, 2013 MEETINGS BETWEEN THE PARTIES. (DECL. OF WOO IN SUPPORT OF MOTION, 17-21.) JENNIFER HOVLAND'S ABSENCE FROM THE SETTLEMENT NEGOTIATIONS ON OCTOBER 30, 2012, DOES NOT RENDER THE SETTLEMENT UNENFORCEABLE. ALL LITIGANTS TO THE ACTION SIGNED THE WRITTEN AGREEMENT. SEE CORTEZ V. KENNEALLY (1996) 44 CAL.APP.4TH 523. THIS ALSO INCLUDES THE RETURN OF PHOTOGRAPHS TO PLAINTIFF. COMPLIANCE DATE IS SET BY THE COURT ON OR BEFORE THE END OF BUSINESS DAY OF JUNE 28, 2013 TO TAKE PLACE AT THE LAW OFFICE OF MR. SHEPPARD AND IF NOT SUCCESFUL, PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL MAY APPLY FOR AN ORDER FOR FURTHER RELIEF. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. CROSS DEFENDANT EDWIN LOW, CONNIE LOW YAMAMOTO AND JENNIFER HOVLAND MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITH FIFTEEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE PRAYER FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF AND DENIED AS TO THE REFERENCES TO THE 25 BANK ACCOUNTS. THE RELIEF SOUGHT IN THE PRAYER FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF GOES WELL BEYOND WHAT IS AVAILABLE FOR AN INJUNCTION TO ABATE A NUISANCE, WHICH IS THE ONLY BASIS THAT SUSANNE SAYS SHE IS SEEKING INJUNCTIVE RELIEF. WITHOUT ADDITIONAL FACTS, IT IS NOT POSSIBLE TO DETERMINE WHETHER SUSANNE'S ALLEGATIONS AS TO ANY OR ALL OF THE ALLEGED 25 BANK ACCOUNTS MUST BE RAISED IN THE PROBATE DIVISION OR MAY BE ASSERTED IN A CIVIL ACTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR #5976

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-24-2013 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion To Enforce Settlement And For Entry Of Judgment Against Susanne Low [Ccp 664.6]
Off Calendar Jun-19-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. WARREN T. WOO, TEL. NO. 752-4608 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. ROBERT J. SHEPPARD OF SHEPPARD, UZIEL, SUSSMAN & ROSEN, TEL. NO. 296-0900 EXT. 23 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS,
RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFFS EDWIN LOW, CONNIE LOW YAMAMOTO and JENNIFER HOVLAND's Motion To Enforce Settlement And For Entry Of Judgment Against Susanne Low [CCP 664.6] Motion for judgment and to enforce settlement agreement is granted pursuant to CCP 664.6. The written Stipulation for Settlement was agreed to between the parties and provided that it was "binding and may be enforced by a motion under Code of Civil Procedure 664.6 or by any other procedure permitted by law in the applicable state or federal court." Defendant Suzanne Low failed to comply with the terms of the Stipulation for Settlement when she failed to sign and deliver a grant deed of her 8 and 1/3% interest in property 809 Columbus to Plaintiff Edwin Low. (Decl. of Edwin Low, Exh. A1 - A4.) Plaintiffs satisfied their obligations under the settlement agreement by making an initial $60,000 payment (Decl. of Woo in Support of Motion, 12-19), providing records recorded by Mr. Woo on the property to Mr. Sheppard (Decl. of Woo in Support of Reply to the Opposition, 28), and by bringing a cashiers' check for $100,000 to the December 10, 2012 and March 18, 2013 meetings between the parties. (Decl. of Woo in Support of Motion, 17-21.) Jennifer Hovland's absence from the settlement negotiations on October 30, 2012, does not render the settlement unenforceable. All litigants to the action signed the written agreement. See Cortez v. Kenneally (1996) 44 Cal.App.4th 523. This also includes the return of photographs to Plaintiff. COMPLIANCE DATE IS SET BY THE COURT ON OR BEFORE THE END OF BUSINESS DAY OF JUNE 28, 2013 TO TAKE PLACE AT THE LAW OFFICE OF MR. SHEPPARD AND IF NOT SUCCESFUL, PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL MAY APPLY FOR AN ORDER FOR FURTHER RELIEF. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-04-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. WARREN T. WOO, TEL. NO. 752-4608 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANTS EDWIN LOW, CONNIE LOW YAMAMOTO AND JENNIFER HOVLAND'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is Sustained without leave to amend as to the ninth cause of action for nuisance. Susanne has not adequately pled nuisance based on her allegations of being locked out. Phillips v. City of Pasadena (1945) 27 Cal.2d 104 is distinguishable as it involved access to a road. Nor is there a reasonable possibility that Susanne can cure this deficiency by an amendment. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =302/HK

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-16-2012 Continued To Oct-10-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-17-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Warren T. Woo, Esq., Tel. 415.752.4608, appearing for cross-defendants and moving party;
Juliana Pisani, Esq. of Sheppard Rosen Uziel & Sussman, Tel. 415.296.0900 x28, appearing for defendants.

The matter was argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT EDWIN LOW, CONNIE LOW YAMAMOTO and JENNIFER HOVLAND MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is Granted with fifteen days leave to amend as to the prayer for injunctive relief and denied as to the references to the 25 bank accounts. The relief sought in the prayer for injunctive relief goes well beyond what is available for an injunction to abate a nuisance, which is the only basis that Susanne says she is seeking injunctive relief. Without additional facts, it is not possible to determine whether Susanne's allegations as to any or all of the alleged 25 bank accounts must be raised in the probate division or may be asserted in a civil action. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR #5976

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-17-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Warren T. Woo, Esq., Tel. 415.752.4608, appearing for cross-defendants and moving party;
Juliana Pisani, Esq. of Sheppard Rosen Uziel & Sussman, Tel. 415.296.0900 x28, appearing for defendants.

The matter was argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANTs EDWIN LOW, CONNIE LOW YAMAMOTO and JENNIFER HOVLAND'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is Sustained with fifteen days leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action for waste and overruled as to all other causes of action challenged by the demurrer. Susanne is granted to leave to allege, if she can do so in good faith, that Edwin substantially or permanently diminished or depreciated the market value of the property. All other causes of action are adequately pled and are not uncertain. The court takes judicial notice of the existence and filing of the documents sought to be judicially noticed, but not the truth of the contents of those documents. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR #5976
